scope: Research backlog for the AI agents taxonomy GitHub Pages repository. This JSON catalogs selected sources only; rejected source classes are documented in rejected.md.

sources.title: Building Effective Agents
sources.authors_or_org: Erik Schluntz and Barry Zhang, Anthropic
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Primary practitioner source for workflow-vs-agent, augmented LLM, and the five workflow patterns.
sources.closes_gap: Prevents treating every multi-step LLM system as an agent.
sources.why_prefer: More primary than framework retellings of the same pattern set.
sources.actuality: Still current as a foundational 2024 practitioner framing; verify against newer framework docs before claiming consensus.

sources.title: Writing Tools for Agents
sources.type: official engineering blog / tool design case study
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Supports the claim that tool names, schemas, descriptions, and outputs are part of an agent's reasoning surface.
sources.closes_gap: Explains why tool design is an agent architecture concern rather than a thin API wrapper.
sources.why_prefer: Primary production lesson from a frontier lab.
sources.actuality: Fresh 2025 production guidance.

sources.title: How We Built Our Multi-Agent Research System
sources.type: official engineering blog / production case study
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Production case for supervisor-worker research agents, task decomposition, parallelization, and cost trade-offs.
sources.closes_gap: Replaces generic multi-agent optimism with a task-conditional case.
sources.why_prefer: Gives concrete architecture and operational trade-offs.
sources.actuality: Strong 2025 case study; scope is broad research, not all agent tasks.

sources.title: A Practical Guide to Building Agents
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Independent OpenAI source for incremental agent design, tool selection, guardrails, and orchestration.
sources.closes_gap: Reduces dependence on Anthropic for workflow-vs-agent framing.
sources.why_prefer: Official, implementation-oriented source rather than sales material.
sources.actuality: 2025 guide; exact date should be verified before citation.

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Documentation
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Current OpenAI vocabulary for agents, tools, handoffs, and guardrails.
sources.closes_gap: Keeps OpenAI product placement tied to living docs.
sources.why_prefer: Official API docs are stronger than third-party tutorials.
sources.actuality: Living docs; re-check on page edit.

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Tracing Documentation
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Concrete source for agent traces, spans, model calls, tool calls, and handoffs.
sources.closes_gap: Moves observability beyond generic logs.
sources.actuality: Living docs; re-check on page edit.

sources.title: OpenAI Computer Use Tool Documentation
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Primary current source for computer-use tool loops, screenshots, actions, and external-interface interaction.
sources.closes_gap: Grounds computer use in official current docs instead of demos.
sources.why_prefer: Official docs are more durable for current capability shape than media coverage.
sources.actuality: Living docs; re-check before page edits.

sources.title: Operator System Card
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Primary source for confirmations, restricted actions, monitoring, and adversarial browser-agent risks.
sources.closes_gap: Supports HITL and irreversible-action gates.
sources.actuality: 2025 system card; keep dated.

sources.title: PaperBench
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Benchmark source for long-horizon scientific implementation tasks.
sources.closes_gap: Adds long-horizon artifact evaluation beyond short QA.
sources.actuality: 2025 benchmark; cite design, not current scores.

sources.title: BrowseComp
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Benchmark source for hard web browsing and deep research tasks.
sources.closes_gap: Separates browsing/search competence from generic model capability.
sources.actuality: 2025 benchmark; cite design cautiously.

sources.title: Announcing the Agent2Agent Protocol
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Launch source for A2A's agent-to-agent interoperability scope.
sources.closes_gap: Distinguishes A2A from MCP rather than treating protocols as one layer.
sources.actuality: Launch-era; pair with current A2A spec and 2026 LF update.

sources.title: A2A Protocol Specification
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Primary source for A2A agent cards, tasks, messages, artifacts, streaming, and security.
sources.closes_gap: Prevents stale A2A claims based only on launch posts.
sources.actuality: Current as of 2026-06-03; re-check version before page edit.

sources.title: A2A Protocol Surpasses 150 Organizations, Lands in Major Cloud Platforms, and Sees Enterprise Production Use in First Year
sources.type: official press release / governance and adoption update
sources.reliability: primary for project status; weak for comparative adoption
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Updates A2A status to v1.0.0 and Linux Foundation project activity.
sources.closes_gap: Makes 2026 A2A claims current.
sources.actuality: Fresh 2026 source; do not use alone for MCP-vs-A2A comparisons.

sources.title: Model Context Protocol Specification
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Primary source for MCP primitives and scope.
sources.closes_gap: Clarifies protocol layer using exact spec terms.
sources.why_prefer: Official spec is stronger than launch metaphors.
sources.actuality: Dated spec; re-check latest version before page edit.

sources.title: LangChain Agent Protocol
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Shows protocol scope as runtime control APIs for running agents.
sources.closes_gap: Prevents protocol section from being MCP-vs-A2A only.
sources.actuality: Living repo; verify activity before page edit.

sources.title: AGNTCY Documentation
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Source for identity, directory, discovery, and broader interoperability concerns.
sources.closes_gap: Extends protocol layer beyond message transport and tool context.
sources.actuality: Living docs; verify current project status before page edit.

sources.title: OpenTelemetry GenAI Semantic Conventions
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Vendor-neutral vocabulary for tracing GenAI operations.
sources.closes_gap: Adds standards-backed observability source.
sources.why_prefer: Standards project rather than product docs.
sources.actuality: Living spec; re-check stability before page edit.

sources.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Foundational source for reasoning/action interleaving.
sources.closes_gap: Grounds tool-using LLM agents in research.
sources.actuality: Foundational; still relevant.

sources.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Source for evaluator/self-feedback loops and feedback memory.
sources.closes_gap: Adds basis for critic/evaluator cycles.
sources.actuality: Useful 2023 pattern source; pair with newer evals.

sources.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Concrete architecture for memory stream, reflection, planning, and social interaction.
sources.closes_gap: Separates episodic memory/reflection from generic RAG.
sources.actuality: Foundational LLM-era social-agent source.

sources.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Source for managed memory hierarchy between context and external storage.
sources.closes_gap: Prevents memory from being reduced to prompt length or vector search.
sources.actuality: Still relevant for memory architecture.

sources.title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
sources.authors_or_org: Qingyun Wu et al., Microsoft Research
sources.type: paper / preprint; official Microsoft project source
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Framework source for conversation-based multi-agent orchestration.
sources.closes_gap: Adds multi-agent framework evidence beyond Anthropic.
sources.actuality: Important early framework source; verify current AutoGen docs separately.

sources.title: Magentic-One: A Generalist Multi-Agent System
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Architecture source for orchestrator plus specialized agents.
sources.closes_gap: Supports roles in a coordination graph with an independent Microsoft system.
sources.actuality: Fresh 2024 research system.

sources.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Benchmark source for interactive agent environments.
sources.closes_gap: Adds task-success evaluation beyond static QA.
sources.actuality: Use benchmark design, not transient scores.

sources.title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Benchmark source for realistic web-agent tasks.
sources.closes_gap: Moves web-agent claims away from demos.
sources.actuality: Use task design, not leaderboard scores.

sources.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Coding-agent benchmark based on real GitHub issues and tests.
sources.closes_gap: Supports coding-agent evaluation and regression suite language.
sources.actuality: Use benchmark design, not current top score.

sources.title: GAIA: A Benchmark for General AI Assistants
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Benchmark for mixed reasoning, tool use, web browsing, and assistant tasks.
sources.closes_gap: Supports evaluation across mixed skills.
sources.actuality: Use task categories, not leaderboard numbers.

sources.title: tau-bench: Tool-Agent-User Interaction Benchmark
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Benchmark for tool-agent-user interactions under policies and state.
sources.closes_gap: Adds operational tool-use benchmark beyond web/coding.
sources.actuality: Fresh 2024 preprint; verify publication status before formal citation.

sources.title: OSWorld: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Open-Ended Tasks in Real Computer Environments
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Benchmark source for desktop/computer-use agents.
sources.closes_gap: Distinguishes GUI-level action from API tool calls.
sources.why_prefer: Stronger than vendor demos for computer-use capability claims.
sources.actuality: Use benchmark design, not transient scores.

sources.title: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Benchmark evidence for prompt injection and tool-use attacks in agents.
sources.closes_gap: Supports treating tool outputs and retrieved content as untrusted.
sources.actuality: Fresh 2024 security benchmark.

sources.title: Agent-SafetyBench
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Broader safety-evaluation source for agent systems.
sources.closes_gap: Expands safety evidence beyond prompt injection.
sources.why_prefer: Structured benchmark, but should be paired with OWASP/system cards.
sources.actuality: Fresh preprint; verify status before citation.

sources.title: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Security vocabulary for prompt injection, excessive agency, data leakage, and supply-chain risk.
sources.closes_gap: Adds formal security categories to failure modes.
sources.actuality: Current for 2025 LLM app security; pair with agent-specific OWASP guidance.

sources.title: OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Agentic execution-layer risk framing around skills, tool authority, manifests, audit logging, and workflow behavior.
sources.closes_gap: Adds agent-specific security controls for skills and tool execution.
sources.why_prefer: More relevant to coding-agent and skill execution systems than generic prompt-injection explainers.
sources.actuality: Fresh 2026 OWASP project; verify current status before page edit.

sources.title: Careful Adoption of Agentic AI Services
sources.authors_or_org: Five Eyes cybersecurity agencies, published by New Zealand NCSC
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Fresh official guidance for permissions, monitoring, procurement, and controls.
sources.closes_gap: Adds independent public-sector evidence for high-risk deployment.
sources.actuality: Very fresh 2026 source.

sources.title: Intelligent Agents: Theory and Practice
sources.authors_or_org: Michael Wooldridge and Nicholas R. Jennings
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Classic source for autonomy, social ability, reactivity, and proactiveness.
sources.closes_gap: Shows that agent vocabulary predates LLM tooling.
sources.actuality: Foundational; not a current LLM implementation source.

sources.title: Agent-Oriented Programming
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Classic source for agent-oriented abstractions and mental-state vocabulary.
sources.closes_gap: Adds historical grounding for roles, commitments, and intentions.
sources.actuality: Foundational; transfer cautiously to LLM systems.

sources.title: The Contract Net Protocol: High-Level Communication and Control in a Distributed Problem Solver
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Classic task allocation protocol for announcement, bidding, and award.
sources.closes_gap: Shows delegation and market-style allocation predate LLM agents.
sources.actuality: Foundational coordination protocol.

sources.title: BDI Agents: From Theory to Practice
sources.authors_or_org: Anand S. Rao and Michael P. Georgeff
sources.role_for_taxonomy: Classic source for beliefs, desires, intentions, commitments, and plan selection.
sources.closes_gap: Adds formal background for autonomy and plan language without anthropomorphism.
sources.actuality: Foundational; not a current LLM-agent source.
